explanations.1: According to the prelude, special relativity deals with constant velocity frames, whereas general relativity addresses acceleration.
explanations.2: The text states that relativistic mechanics becomes the same as classical mechanics for objects moving slower than about 1% of the speed of light.
explanations.3: The text defines relativity as the study of how different observers measure the same event.
explanations.4: An inertial frame is one in which Newton’s first law, the law of inertia, holds exactly.
explanations.5: The first postulate asserts that the laws of physics are identical in all inertial frames.
explanations.6: The second postulate states that the speed of light is constant and independent of the motion of the source or observer.
explanations.7: The Michelson-Morley experiment demonstrated that the speed of light is independent of the Earth’s motion, implying no medium is needed.
explanations.8: Maxwell’s equations predict a constant speed \(c\), which contradicts the classical Newtonian addition of velocities.
explanations.9: Simultaneity is not absolute; relative motion between observers affects whether two events are seen as occurring at the same time.
explanations.10: Time dilation describes the lengthening of time intervals for a system in motion relative to an observer.
explanations.11: Proper time is defined as the time interval measured by an observer who is at rest relative to the process.
explanations.13: Using \(\Delta t = \gamma \Delta t_{0}\), the observed time is \(3.20 \times 1.52 \, \mu s = 4.864 \, \mu s\).
explanations.14: The traveling twin is not in a single inertial frame because they must accelerate and decelerate, breaking the symmetry of the frames.
explanations.15: Proper length is measured by an observer at rest relative to the distance being measured.
explanations.16: Length contraction is only observed in the dimension parallel to the relative motion of the observers.
explanations.17: \(L = L_{0} / \gamma = 100 / 1.67 \approx 59.9\) m. Using the exact \(\gamma\) for \(0.8c\) (which is \(1.666...\)) yields exactly 60 m.
explanations.18: To an electron at SLAC, the 3 km accelerator is length-contracted to about 0.5 m due to the electron's relativistic speed.
explanations.19: Classical addition can result in speeds greater than \(c\), which violates the second postulate of special relativity.
explanations.21: A red shift occurs when the observed wavelength of electromagnetic radiation is longer than the source wavelength because the source is receding.
explanations.22: As shown in the exercise, \(f_{obs} = 1.50 \sqrt{(1 - 0.35) / (1 + 0.35)} \approx 1.04\) GHz.
explanations.23: Relativistic momentum is defined as \(p = \gamma mu\), which ensures conservation of momentum in all inertial frames.
explanations.24: The text notes that the only meaningful, independently determined mass is the rest mass \(m\).
explanations.26: Einstein’s equation \(E_{0} = mc^{2}\) shows that mass itself is a form of energy even when an object is at rest.
explanations.28: As \(v\) approaches \(c\), \(\gamma\) and kinetic energy approach infinity, requiring infinite energy to reach the speed of light.
